items.subtopic: ASSESSMENT OF LEFT VENTRICULAR DIASTOLIC FUNCTION
items.statement: Diastolic function parameters need to be adjusted for age.
items.explanation: Diastolic function worsens with aging, and most diastolic parameters need to be adjusted for age.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · ASSESSMENT OF LEFT VENTRICULAR DIASTOLIC FUNCTION

items.statement: The McConnell sign is specific for acute myocardial infarction.
items.explanation: The McConnell sign is specific for acute pulmonary embolism, not acute myocardial infarction.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · ASSESSMENT OF RIGHT VENTRICULAR FUNCTION

items.statement: Right ventricular dysfunction can be a hallmark of severe COVID-19 disease.
items.explanation: Severe COVID-19 disease can lead to right ventricular dysfunction due to macro- or microthrombosis in the pulmonary vasculature.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · ASSESSMENT OF RIGHT VENTRICULAR FUNCTION

items.statement: The effective dose from a typical myocardial perfusion PET scan is higher than that of a SPECT scan.
items.explanation: The effective dose from a typical myocardial perfusion PET scan (~2.0–4 mSv) is lower than that of a SPECT scan.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · RADIATION EXPOSURE

items.statement: Radiation exposure from natural sources in the United States amounts to ~3 mSv annually.
items.explanation: Exposure to radiation from natural sources in the United States is approximately 3 mSv per year.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · RADIATION EXPOSURE

items.statement: The risk of contrast-induced nephropathy (CIN) is high in patients with estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) >60 mL/min.
items.explanation: The risk of CIN is low in patients with relatively normal renal function (eGFR >60 mL/min).
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · CONTRAST AGENTS

items.statement: Group II macrocyclic-structured GBCAs are considered safe for use in patients with severe renal dysfunction.
items.explanation: The American College of Radiology considers using group II agents as safe in patients with severe renal dysfunction or on dialysis.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · CONTRAST AGENTS

items.subtopic: SAFETY CONSIDERATIONS OF CMR IN PATIENTS WITH PACEMAKERS AND DEFIBRILLATORS | CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 1)
items.statement: Stress echocardiography can be performed with exercise or dobutamine stress.
items.explanation: Stress echocardiography can indeed be performed in conjunction with either exercise or dobutamine stress.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · SAFETY CONSIDERATIONS OF CMR IN PATIENTS WITH PACEMAKERS AND DEFIBRILLATORS | CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 1)

items.subtopic: SAFETY CONSIDERATIONS OF CMR IN PATIENTS WITH PACEMAKERS AND DEFIBRILLATORS | CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 1)
items.statement: A negative SPECT or PET study indicates a high rate of major adverse cardiac events.
items.explanation: Patients with a normal SPECT or PET study exhibit a low rate of major adverse cardiac events, less than 1% annually.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · SAFETY CONSIDERATIONS OF CMR IN PATIENTS WITH PACEMAKERS AND DEFIBRILLATORS | CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 1)

items.statement: CAC scores <400 are effective in excluding CAD, especially in young symptomatic patients with primarily noncalcified atherosclerosis.
items.explanation: CAC scores <400 may be less effective in excluding CAD, particularly in young symptomatic individuals who might have primarily noncalcified atherosclerosis.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 2)

items.statement: A normal coronary CT angiography scan result effectively excludes obstructive CAD.
items.explanation: The high negative predictive value of CTA means a normal scan result effectively excludes obstructive CAD.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 2)

items.statement: Stress CMR-guided strategy has been shown to improve outcomes by guiding invasive investigation and coronary revascularization.
items.explanation: A recent randomized controlled trial demonstrated that a stress CMR-guided strategy improved guidance toward the use of invasive investigation and coronary revascularization.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 3)

items.statement: Exercise testing is equally effective for diagnosing obstructive CAD in men and women.
items.explanation: The use of exercise testing presents difficulties in women due to lower prevalence of obstructive CAD, different accuracy compared to men, and other factors.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 3)

items.statement: Coronary CTA provides excellent visualization of the native circulation in patients with prior revascularization.
items.explanation: In patients with prior revascularization, the native circulation tends to get heavily calcified and is generally not a good target for imaging with coronary CTA.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 4)

items.statement: Multiparametric CMR imaging can diagnose conditions that mimic ACS.
items.explanation: CMR is useful in diagnosing conditions like acute myocarditis, takotsubo cardiomyopathy, and pericarditis, which can mimic ACS.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 4)

items.statement: Patients with very low clinical risk and negative biomarkers can be safely triaged without specialized imaging testing.
items.explanation: The passage suggests that patients with very low clinical risk and negative biomarkers do not require specialized imaging.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 5)

items.statement: All studies reported cost savings when using coronary CTA.
items.explanation: The passage notes that most but not all studies reported cost savings with coronary CTA.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 5)

items.statement: A bicuspid aortic valve is less common than a tricuspid aortic valve.
items.explanation: Bicuspid aortic valve is the most common congenital anomaly, making it more common than the normal tricuspid configuration.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · VALVULAR HEART DISEASE (part 1)

items.statement: Aortic valve areas less than 0.6 cm² are considered moderate aortic stenosis.
items.explanation: Aortic valve areas less than 0.6 cm² are considered critical, not moderate.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · VALVULAR HEART DISEASE (part 1)

items.statement: Aortic regurgitation can result from dilatation of the aortic root even if the valve leaflets are intrinsically normal.
items.explanation: Dilatation of the aortic root can lead to aortic regurgitation due to malcoaptation of the leaflets, even when they are intrinsically normal.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · VALVULAR HEART DISEASE (part 2)

items.statement: The vena contracta is used to assess ventricular size and function in patients with aortic regurgitation.
items.explanation: The vena contracta is used to assess the severity of aortic regurgitation, not ventricular size or function.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · VALVULAR HEART DISEASE (part 2)

items.statement: Ejection fraction in patients with severe mitral regurgitation accurately reflects myocardial systolic function.
items.explanation: In severe mitral regurgitation, the ejection fraction may be artificially increased due to regurgitant flow.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · VALVULAR HEART DISEASE (part 3)

items.statement: Echocardiography can assess left atrial size and right ventricular function in mitral stenosis.
items.explanation: Echocardiography is useful for assessing these parameters to determine the severity of mitral stenosis.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · VALVULAR HEART DISEASE (part 3)

items.statement: Microvascular obstruction can be seen as dense hypoenhanced areas within the core of a bright region of infarction.
items.explanation: Microvascular obstruction is visualized as dense hypoenhanced areas in LGE imaging.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · MYOCARDIAL INFARCTION AND HEART FAILURE (part 1)

items.statement: Echocardiography should be used immediately after myocardial infarction to accurately assess cardiac function.
items.explanation: Early echocardiographic assessment may be misleading due to myocardial stunning; imaging is often deferred for several days.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · MYOCARDIAL INFARCTION AND HEART FAILURE (part 1)

items.statement: PET metabolic imaging can be used to monitor therapeutic response in patients with inflammatory cardiomyopathies.
items.explanation: PET is frequently used to monitor therapeutic response in inflammatory cardiomyopathies like sarcoidosis.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · MYOCARDIAL INFARCTION AND HEART FAILURE (part 2)

items.statement: Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy typically shows decreased ventricular thickness and no outflow obstruction.
items.explanation: Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy is characterized by increased ventricular thickness and often has outflow obstruction.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · MYOCARDIAL INFARCTION AND HEART FAILURE (part 2)

items.subtopic: ASSESSING CARDIAC FUNCTION IN PATIENTS UNDERGOING CANCER TREATMENT
items.statement: Early recognition of cardiomyocyte injury is crucial for the successful application of preventative therapies.
items.explanation: Early recognition and treatment are critical due to late manifestation of adverse effects.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · ASSESSING CARDIAC FUNCTION IN PATIENTS UNDERGOING CANCER TREATMENT

items.subtopic: ASSESSING CARDIAC FUNCTION IN PATIENTS UNDERGOING CANCER TREATMENT
items.statement: Molecular imaging approaches targeting mechanisms of cardiac toxicity are currently standard practice.
items.explanation: These molecular imaging techniques are considered experimental and not yet standard.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · ASSESSING CARDIAC FUNCTION IN PATIENTS UNDERGOING CANCER TREATMENT

items.statement: A lack of thickened pericardium rules out pericardial constriction.
items.explanation: Approximately one in five patients with severe pericardial constriction have no significant pericardial thickening.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · PERICARDIAL DISEASE

items.statement: Rapid accumulation of pericardial fluid can lead to tamponade physiology without very large effusions.
items.explanation: Rapid accumulations, such as those from cardiac rupture or perforation, can cause tamponade without large effusions.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · PERICARDIAL DISEASE

items.statement: Cardiac CT imaging is ideal for detecting small thrombus in the left atrial appendage.
items.explanation: Cardiac CT is suited for small thrombus detection, especially when transesophageal echocardiography is suboptimal.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · CARDIAC THROMBUS AND MASS

items.statement: Primary malignant cardiac tumors are more common than primary benign cardiac tumors.
items.explanation: Primary malignant cardiac tumors are rare compared to primary benign cardiac tumors.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · CARDIAC THROMBUS AND MASS

items.subtopic: ROLE OF IMAGING IN INFECTIOUS AND INFLAMMATORY DISEASE | EVALUATION OF COMMON CONGENITAL ABNORMALITIES IN THE ADULT
items.statement: The absence of a vegetation on echocardiography definitively rules out endocarditis.
items.explanation: Small vegetations may be below the resolution of imaging techniques, so their absence does not rule out endocarditis.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · ROLE OF IMAGING IN INFECTIOUS AND INFLAMMATORY DISEASE | EVALUATION OF COMMON CONGENITAL ABNORMALITIES IN THE ADULT

items.subtopic: ROLE OF IMAGING IN INFECTIOUS AND INFLAMMATORY DISEASE | EVALUATION OF COMMON CONGENITAL ABNORMALITIES IN THE ADULT
items.statement: Atrial septal defects are best diagnosed using agitated saline in all cases.
items.explanation: Color flow Doppler is usually sufficient for secundum-type atrial septal defects, but agitated saline is needed for other types.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · ROLE OF IMAGING IN INFECTIOUS AND INFLAMMATORY DISEASE | EVALUATION OF COMMON CONGENITAL ABNORMALITIES IN THE ADULT

items.statement: AI can reduce the complexities involved in cardiac MRI image acquisition.
items.explanation: AI solutions guide cardiac MRI scanning, reducing complexities of image acquisition and reconstruction.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE IN CARDIAC IMAGING

items.statement: The use of AI in echocardiography eliminates the need for a probe.
items.explanation: AI directs users to obtain optimal images but does not eliminate the need for a probe.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE IN CARDIAC IMAGING

items.subtopic: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging: Echocardiography, Nuclear Cardiology, and Magnetic Resonance/Computed Tomography Imaging
items.assertion: Cardiac imaging has been one of the greatest advances in cardiovascular medicine.
items.reason: It allows noninvasive imaging of the heart and blood vessels.
items.explanation: The assertion is true, and the reason correctly explains why it is considered a great advance.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging: Echocardiography, Nuclear Cardiology, and Magnetic Resonance/Computed Tomography Imaging

items.assertion: The spatial resolution of ultrasound is dependent on the frequency.
items.reason: Higher frequencies provide better spatial resolution but less penetration.
items.explanation: Both statements are true, and the reason correctly explains the assertion.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · ECHOCARDIOGRAPHY (part 1)

items.assertion: Continuous wave Doppler can assess blood flow velocity at specific locations within the heart.
items.reason: It is used to interrogate high velocity flows but cannot determine depth-specific velocities.
items.explanation: The assertion is false; continuous wave Doppler cannot assess specific location velocities.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · ECHOCARDIOGRAPHY (part 1)

items.assertion: A normal myocardial perfusion study following a rest injection effectively excludes myocardial ischemia as the cause of chest pain.
items.reason: Technetium-99m tracers become trapped intracellularly and show minimal change over time, allowing imaging after symptoms subside.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains why a normal study excludes ischemia.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · RADIONUCLIDE IMAGING (part 1)

items.assertion: Dobutamine is used as an alternative to vasodilator stress in patients with chronic pulmonary disease.
items.reason: Vasodilators are contraindicated in patients with chronic pulmonary disease due to their effects on the respiratory system.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason is a correct explanation for using dobutamine instead of vasodilators.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · RADIONUCLIDE IMAGING (part 1)

items.assertion: CT calcium scoring is associated with high cardiac prognostic value.
items.reason: Population-based studies in asymptomatic cohorts have reported this association.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains why CT calcium scoring has high cardiac prognostic value.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · RADIONUCLIDE IMAGING (part 2) | CARDIAC COMPUTED TOMOGRAPHY

items.assertion: Respiratory motion is reduced by using beta blockade in coronary CTA.
items.reason: Beta blockade slows the patient's heart rate, reducing cardiac motion during imaging.
items.explanation: The assertion is false because respiratory motion is reduced by breathholding, not beta blockade. The reason is true as it explains how beta blockade reduces cardiac motion.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · RADIONUCLIDE IMAGING (part 2) | CARDIAC COMPUTED TOMOGRAPHY

items.subtopic: ASSESSMENT OF CARDIAC STRUCTURE AND FUNCTION
items.assertion: Left ventricular dilatation is common to several cardiac diseases.
items.reason: Regional dysfunction secondary to myocardial infarction can lead to progressive ventricular dilatation or remodeling.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains why left ventricular dilatation is common in several cardiac diseases.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · ASSESSMENT OF CARDIAC STRUCTURE AND FUNCTION

items.subtopic: ASSESSMENT OF CARDIAC STRUCTURE AND FUNCTION
items.assertion: Echocardiography requires geometric assumptions for accurate volumetric assessment.
items.reason: High-resolution tomographic techniques such as CMR or cardiac CT are more accurate for volumetric assessment.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, but the reason does not explain why echocardiography requires geometric assumptions.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · ASSESSMENT OF CARDIAC STRUCTURE AND FUNCTION

items.subtopic: ASSESSMENT OF LEFT VENTRICULAR SYSTOLIC FUNCTION
items.assertion: Myocardial strain imaging is increasingly utilized for early detection of cardiotoxicity.
items.reason: It provides a sensitive approach to detecting systolic dysfunction without geometric assumptions.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason explains why myocardial strain imaging is used in this context.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · ASSESSMENT OF LEFT VENTRICULAR SYSTOLIC FUNCTION

items.subtopic: ASSESSMENT OF LEFT VENTRICULAR DIASTOLIC FUNCTION
items.assertion: Mitral annular relaxation velocity (E') has prognostic significance in patients with heart failure.
items.reason: E' is inversely related to the time constant of relaxation, tau.
items.explanation: Both E' having prognostic significance and its inverse relationship with tau are true, but the reason does not explain the assertion.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · ASSESSMENT OF LEFT VENTRICULAR DIASTOLIC FUNCTION

items.assertion: Right ventricular dilatation can occur due to chronic processes like long-standing pulmonary hypertension.
items.reason: Chronic pulmonary hypertension leads to right ventricular hypertrophy and ultimately dilatation.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains the assertion.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · ASSESSMENT OF RIGHT VENTRICULAR FUNCTION

items.assertion: Left atrial enlargement is a marker of systolic dysfunction.
items.reason: Left atrial size reflects long-standing increases in left-sided filling pressures.
items.explanation: The assertion is false because left atrial enlargement indicates diastolic dysfunction, not systolic.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · ASSESSMENT OF RIGHT VENTRICULAR FUNCTION

items.assertion: Nephrogenic systemic fibrosis (NSF) is a risk factor associated with the use of group I GBCAs.
items.reason: Group I linear-structured GBCAs have been linked to NSF, especially in patients with severe renal dysfunction.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains the assertion.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · CONTRAST AGENTS

items.subtopic: SAFETY CONSIDERATIONS OF CMR IN PATIENTS WITH PACEMAKERS AND DEFIBRILLATORS | CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 1)
items.assertion: Stress echocardiography is limited by high operator dependence.
items.reason: Acquisition of echocardiographic data and analysis of images are highly dependent on the skill of the operator.
items.explanation: Both the assertion that stress echocardiography has limitations due to high operator dependence and the reason explaining this limitation are true, with the latter correctly explaining the former.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · SAFETY CONSIDERATIONS OF CMR IN PATIENTS WITH PACEMAKERS AND DEFIBRILLATORS | CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 1)

items.subtopic: SAFETY CONSIDERATIONS OF CMR IN PATIENTS WITH PACEMAKERS AND DEFIBRILLATORS | CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 1)
items.assertion: Stress radionuclide imaging is the most common form of stress imaging tests for CAD evaluation.
items.reason: PET has advantages compared to SPECT and is more commonly used in clinical practice.
items.explanation: The assertion that SPECT myocardial perfusion imaging is the most common form of stress imaging tests for CAD evaluation is true, but the reason is false because PET is not more widely used than SPECT.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · SAFETY CONSIDERATIONS OF CMR IN PATIENTS WITH PACEMAKERS AND DEFIBRILLATORS | CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 1)

items.assertion: Quantitative myocardial blood flow and coronary flow reserve with PET can help mitigate the limitations of radionuclide imaging.
items.reason: Radionuclide imaging often uncovers only coronary territories supplied by the most severe stenoses.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains why PET can mitigate these limitations.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 2)

items.assertion: The presence of nonobstructive atherosclerosis does not increase the risk of adverse cardiac events.
items.reason: New evidence suggests that even nonobstructive atherosclerosis increases the risk of adverse cardiac events.
items.explanation: The assertion is false, while the reason is true; new evidence indicates that nonobstructive atherosclerosis does increase the risk.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 2)

items.assertion: Stress imaging is preferred over standard stress testing for diagnosing obstructive CAD in women.
items.reason: The lower pretest probability of disease and higher false positive rates make exercise testing less accurate in women.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, but recent data suggest that ETT is effective for symptomatic, low-risk women who can exercise.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 3)

items.assertion: Dobutamine CMR has better diagnostic accuracy than dobutamine echocardiography in patients with poor acoustic window.
items.reason: Better delineation of endocardial borders is achieved with dobutamine CMR.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains why dobutamine CMR has better diagnostic accuracy.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 3)

items.assertion: Stress imaging approaches are preferred in symptomatic patients with established CAD.
items.reason: Normal imaging studies identify a low-risk cohort, and abnormal studies relate to posttest risk.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains why stress imaging is preferred.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 4)

items.assertion: Coronary CTA is effective in excluding obstructive CAD in all patients.
items.reason: It has high accuracy to define stenosis severity and predict ischemia.
items.explanation: The assertion is false because coronary CTA is less effective in patients with intermediate or higher pretest risk, while the reason is also false as it inaccurately describes CTA's capabilities.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE (part 4)

items.assertion: Echocardiography is the most cost-effective screening method for valvular heart disease.
items.reason: Echocardiography provides a comprehensive assessment of all valves and serves as an adequate screening test for significant valvular disease.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains why echocardiography is cost-effective.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · VALVULAR HEART DISEASE (part 1)

items.assertion: Patients with severe aortic stenosis always require immediate surgery.
items.reason: Valve areas or gradients alone should not determine the need for aortic valve surgery as it remains a clinical decision.
items.explanation: The assertion is false because the need for surgery depends on various factors, not just severity.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · VALVULAR HEART DISEASE (part 1)

items.assertion: CMR can accurately quantify aortic regurgitant volume better than echocardiography.
items.reason: CMR offers several advantages over echocardiography in the assessment of aortic regurgitation.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains why CMR is superior to echocardiography for quantifying aortic regurgitant volume.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · VALVULAR HEART DISEASE (part 2)

items.assertion: Mitral valve prolapse can be due to papillary muscle rupture or dysfunction following myocardial infarction.
items.reason: Papillary muscle rupture is a common cause of mitral regurgitation.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains that papillary muscle issues can lead to mitral valve prolapse.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · VALVULAR HEART DISEASE (part 2)

items.assertion: Functional mitral regurgitation can be distinguished from intrinsic mitral valve disease.
items.reason: It is generally secondary to apical displacement of the papillary muscles in a dilated ventricle.
items.explanation: The reason correctly explains why functional mitral regurgitation can be distinguished.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · VALVULAR HEART DISEASE (part 3)

items.assertion: Rheumatic mitral stenosis results from severe calcification of the mitral leaflets.
items.reason: It is characterized by tethering at the leaflet tips and relative pliability.
items.explanation: The assertion is false; rheumatic mitral stenosis is due to tethering, not calcification.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · VALVULAR HEART DISEASE (part 3)

items.assertion: Echocardiography provides a direct assessment of ventricular function in new-onset heart failure.
items.reason: It helps distinguish patients with different types of ejection fraction, which is important for therapeutic options.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains why echocardiography is used.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · MYOCARDIAL INFARCTION AND HEART FAILURE (part 1)

items.assertion: Cardiac rupture following myocardial infarction should be treated surgically.
items.reason: The presence of thrombus within the pericardial space indicates cardiac rupture, which is a surgical emergency.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains why surgery is needed for cardiac rupture.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · MYOCARDIAL INFARCTION AND HEART FAILURE (part 1)

items.subtopic: ASSESSING CARDIAC FUNCTION IN PATIENTS UNDERGOING CANCER TREATMENT
items.assertion: Noninvasive imaging is essential for diagnosing cardiac toxicity in cancer patients.
items.reason: Noninvasive imaging techniques like echocardiography and radionuclide angiography play a major role in diagnosis.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, with the reason correctly explaining why noninvasive imaging is essential.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · ASSESSING CARDIAC FUNCTION IN PATIENTS UNDERGOING CANCER TREATMENT

items.assertion: Echocardiography is useful in evaluating pericardial constrictive physiology.
items.reason: A thick noncompliant pericardium impairs cardiac filling.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains the assertion.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · PERICARDIAL DISEASE

items.assertion: CT offers tomographic assessment of pericardial thickening.
items.reason: CT provides detailed information on myocardial adhesions.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, but CT does not specifically provide information on myocardial adhesions.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · PERICARDIAL DISEASE

items.assertion: Gadolinium contrast enhancement can help differentiate a tumor from a thrombus.
items.reason: Increased capillary perfusion detected by gadolinium contrast indicates vascularity within the mass.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains the assertion.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · CARDIAC THROMBUS AND MASS

items.subtopic: ROLE OF IMAGING IN INFECTIOUS AND INFLAMMATORY DISEASE | EVALUATION OF COMMON CONGENITAL ABNORMALITIES IN THE ADULT
items.assertion: PET metabolic imaging is useful in identifying the source of infection in patients with prosthetic valves.
items.reason: Echocardiography and/or blood cultures may be negative, making PET a valuable alternative.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains why PET is useful.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · ROLE OF IMAGING IN INFECTIOUS AND INFLAMMATORY DISEASE | EVALUATION OF COMMON CONGENITAL ABNORMALITIES IN THE ADULT

items.subtopic: ROLE OF IMAGING IN INFECTIOUS AND INFLAMMATORY DISEASE | EVALUATION OF COMMON CONGENITAL ABNORMALITIES IN THE ADULT
items.assertion: The size of a vegetation has no prognostic importance in endocarditis.
items.reason: Vegetations should be measured because their size can determine the need for surgical intervention.
items.explanation: The assertion is false; the size of vegetations does have prognostic importance.
items.reference: Noninvasive Cardiac Imaging Echocardiography Nuclear Cardiology and Ma · ROLE OF IMAGING IN INFECTIOUS AND INFLAMMATORY DISEASE | EVALUATION OF COMMON CONGENITAL ABNORMALITIES IN THE ADULT
